title: Esula cyparissias
intro: Esula cyparissias, also known as Hound's Tongue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Boraginaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Hound's Tongue include alkaloids, such as cyprenine and cypelletine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hound's Tongue is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
